name: MuJoCo (Physics Simulation for Robotics and RL)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); Google DeepMind (acquired from Erin Todorov/OpenAI)

auth.notes: Local library; no auth required since Google made it free in 2021

performance.notes: Highly optimized C physics; thousands of simulated steps per second; GPU batching via MJX

agent_readiness.notes: Physics simulation for robotics and RL. import mujoco. model = mujoco.MjModel.from_xml_path('model.xml'). data = mujoco.MjData(model). mujoco.mj_step(model, data). viewer: with mujoco.viewer.launch_passive(model, data). Gymnasium integration: import gymnasium; env = gymnasium.make('HalfCheetah-v4'). Control: data.ctrl. State: data.qpos, data.qvel. Contact forces: data.cfrc_ext. MJCF XML format for model definition. MJX: JAX-based batched simulation on GPU. dm-control: DeepMind's Python wrapper with tasks.

gotchas: MJCF XML model definition has a steep learning curve
Rendering requires display — headless needs offscreen rendering config
Contact dynamics can be numerically unstable for complex contacts
GPU simulation (MJX) requires JAX and is newer/less documented
best_when: Deep RL research on locomotion, manipulation, dexterous hands
avoid_when: Game-like physics (use PyBullet or Unity ML-Agents)
Soft body simulation (MuJoCo has limited soft body support)
Production robotics without sim-to-real transfer expertise
summary: MuJoCo is the gold-standard physics simulator for robotics RL. MjModel.from_xml_path('model.xml'); mj_step(model, data). Gymnasium integration for RL environments. Viewer for visualization. MJCF XML model format. MJX for JAX-based batched GPU simulation. Apache-2.0 since DeepMind open-sourced in 2021. Used for locomotion, manipulation, dexterous robotics RL research. Standard benchmark environments in Gymnasium.
